feed.title: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository on GitHub

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:sensedia-api-platform] Jenkins Sensedia API Platform Plugin vulnerability exposes unencrypted tokens in its global configuration file
entry.content:
Jenkins Sensedia Api Platform tools Plugin 1.0 stores the Sensedia API Manager integration token unencrypted in its global configuration file com.sensedia.configuration.SensediaApiConfiguration.xml on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

This token can be viewed by users with access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the global configuration form does not mask the token, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture it.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53673
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3551
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-93j6-jcjw-3rwp

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:vaddy-plugin] Jenkins VAddy Plugin vulnerability exposes plaintext keys on its job configuration form
entry.content:
Jenkins VAddy Plugin 1.2.8 and earlier does not mask Vaddy API Auth Keys displayed on the job configuration form, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53669
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3527
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8gp3-m447-gw2v

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:nouvola-divecloud] Jenkins Nouvola DiveCloud Plugin vulnerability does not mask keys on its job configuration form
entry.content:
Jenkins Nouvola DiveCloud Plugin 1.08 and earlier does not mask DiveCloud API Keys and Credentials Encryption Keys displayed on the job configuration form, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53671
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3526
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4v4v-92cx-x4f4

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:nouvola-divecloud] Jenkins Nouvola DiveCloud Plugin vulnerability stores unencrypted credentials
entry.content:
Jenkins Nouvola DiveCloud Plugin 1.08 and earlier stores DiveCloud API Keys and Credentials Encryption Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller, where they can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53670
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3526
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-45hr-8gq6-7f7f

entry.title: [io.jenkins.plugins:kryptowire] Jenkins Kryptowire Plugin vulnerability stores unencrypted Kryptowire API key
entry.content:
Jenkins Kryptowire Plugin 0.2 and earlier stores the Kryptowire API key unencrypted in its global configuration file org.aerogear.kryptowire.GlobalConfigurationImpl.xml on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

This API key can be viewed by users with access to the Jenkins controller file system.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53672
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3525
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-cvg7-767r-w3fq

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:deadmanssnitch] Jenkins Dead Man's Snitch Plugin vulnerability stores tokens in plain text
entry.content:
Jenkins Dead Man's Snitch Plugin 0.1 stores Dead Man's Snitch tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller, where they can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53666
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3524
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5pcv-7v3q-hw8j

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:deadmanssnitch] Jenkins Dead Man's Snitch Plugin vulnerability does not mask tokens
entry.content:
Jenkins Dead Man's Snitch Plugin 0.1 does not mask Dead Man's Snitch tokens displayed on the job configuration form, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53667
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3524
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m248-72rh-cpx4

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:ifttt-build-notifier] Jenkins IFTTT Build Notifier Plugin vulnerability exposes IFTTT Maker Channel Keys
entry.content:
Jenkins IFTTT Build Notifier Plugin 1.2 and earlier stores IFTTT Maker Channel Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These keys can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53662
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3541
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jxwj-qccf-4896

entry.title: [com.apica:ApicaLoadtest] Jenkins Apica Loadtest Plugin vulnerability exposes authentication tokens
entry.content:
Jenkins Apica Loadtest Plugin 1.10 and earlier stores Apica Loadtest LTP authentication tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these tokens, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53665
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3540
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-28j3-hphh-cjr8

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:qmetry-test-management] Jenkins QMetry Test Management Plugin stores unencrypted API keys
entry.content:
QMetry Test Management Plugin 1.13 and earlier stores Qmetry Automation API Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These API keys can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these API keys, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53659
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3532
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p9gh-rpjw-78qg

entry.title: [com.ibm.devops:ibm-cloud-devops] Jenkins IBM Cloud DevOps Plugin vulnerability exposes SonarQube authentication tokens
entry.content:
Jenkins IBM Cloud DevOps Plugin 2.0.16 and earlier stores SonarQube authentication tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53663
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3552
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-pgrx-5f8q-r5mq

entry.title: [com.apica:ApicaLoadtest] Jenkins Apica Loadtest Plugin vulnerability exposes authentication tokens
entry.content:
Jenkins Apica Loadtest Plugin 1.10 and earlier stores Apica Loadtest LTP authentication tokens unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these tokens, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53664
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3540
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8p4-vw42-66gh

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:soapui-pro-functional-testing] Jenkins ReadyAPI Functional Testing Plugin vulnerability exposes secrets
entry.content:
Jenkins ReadyAPI Functional Testing Plugin 1.11 and earlier stores SLM License Access Keys, client secrets, and passwords unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These credentials can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these credentials, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53657
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3556
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r496-x769-f8j4

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:applitools-eyes] Jenkins Applitools Eyes Plugin vulnerable to XSS through its Build page
entry.content:
Jenkins Applitools Eyes Plugin 1.16.5 and earlier does not escape the Applitools URL on the build page.

This results in a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exploitable by attackers with Item/Configure permission.

Applitools Eyes Plugin 1.16.6 rejects Applitools URLs that contain HTML metacharacters.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53658
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3509
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j4wf-9gx8-63f8

entry.title: [io.jenkins.plugins:testsigma] Jenkins Testsigma Test Plan vulnerability exposes API keys via job configuration form
entry.content:
Jenkins Testsigma Test Plan run Plugin stores Testsigma API keys in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

While these API keys are stored encrypted on disk, in Testsigma Test Plan run Plugin 1.6 and earlier, the job configuration form does not mask these API keys, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53661
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3515
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8wp4-r84g-gcmw

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:soapui-pro-functional-testing] Jenkins ReadyAPI Functional Testing Plugin vulnerability stores unencrypted authentication credentials
entry.content:
Jenkins ReadyAPI Functional Testing Plugin 1.11 and earlier stores SLM License Access Keys, client secrets, and passwords unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These credentials can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these credentials, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53656
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3556
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-884f-p57j-f258

entry.title: [org.jenkins.plugins.statistics.gatherer:statistics-gatherer] Jenkins Statistics Gatherer Plugin does not mask AWS Secret Key
entry.content:
Jenkins Statistics Gatherer Plugin 2.0.3 and earlier stores the AWS Secret Key unencrypted in its global configuration file org.jenkins.plugins.statistics.gatherer.StatisticsConfiguration.xml on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

This key can be viewed by users with access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the global configuration form does not mask this key, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture it.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53655
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3554
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-26x3-7jw5-7mg4

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:qmetry-test-management] Jenkins QMetry Test Management Plugin vulnerability exposes API keys
entry.content:
QMetry Test Management Plugin 1.13 and earlier stores Qmetry Automation API Keys unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These API keys can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the job configuration form does not mask these API keys, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53660
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3532
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-962q-84v8-hxhj

entry.title: [org.jenkins.plugins.statistics.gatherer:statistics-gatherer] Jenkins Statistics Gatherer Plugin vulnerability exposes AWS Secret Key
entry.content:
Jenkins Statistics Gatherer Plugin 2.0.3 and earlier stores the AWS Secret Key unencrypted in its global configuration file org.jenkins.plugins.statistics.gatherer.StatisticsConfiguration.xml on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

This key can be viewed by users with access to the Jenkins controller file system.

Additionally, the global configuration form does not mask this key, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture it.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53654
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3554
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3c9f-c64m-h4wc

entry.title: [org.jenkins-ci.plugins:aqua-security-scanner] Jenkins Aqua Security Scanner Plugin vulnerability exposes scanner tokens
entry.content:
Jenkins Aqua Security Scanner Plugin 3.2.8 and earlier stores Scanner Tokens for Aqua API unencrypted in job config.xml files on the Jenkins controller as part of its configuration.

These tokens can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller file system.

As of publication of this advisory, there is no fix.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-53653
  • https://www.jenkins.io/security/advisory/2025-07-09/#SECURITY-3542
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3wgg-3j4j-3f69
